questions.prompt: How do you say 'I am a student' in Croatian?
questions.hint: Think about the first person singular of 'biti'.
questions.explanation: 'Sam' is the first person singular present tense of 'biti' (to be). Ja sam = I am.

questions.prompt: Which greeting is used in the evening?
questions.explanation: Dobra večer = Good evening. Dobro jutro = Good morning. Dobar dan = Good day/afternoon.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Oni ___ iz Hrvatske.'
questions.explanation: 'Oni su' = They are. 'Su' is the 3rd person plural of 'biti'.

questions.prompt: What does 'Odakle ste?' mean?
questions.explanation: Odakle = from where, ste = you are (formal). It's the standard way to ask someone's origin.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ana ___ profesorica.'
questions.explanation: 'Je' is the 3rd person singular of 'biti'. Ana je = Ana is.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'goodbye' in Croatian?
questions.explanation: Doviđenja = goodbye. Hvala = thank you. Molim = please / you're welcome.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'We are from Croatia'?
questions.explanation: 'Smo' is the 1st person plural of 'biti'. Mi smo = We are.

questions.prompt: 'Ti ___ student.' — Choose the correct form of 'biti'.
questions.explanation: 'Si' is the 2nd person singular of 'biti'. Ti si = You are (informal).

questions.prompt: Ja ___ studentica. (to be, 1st person singular)
questions.explanation: 'Sam' is the 1st person singular present tense of 'biti' (to be).

questions.prompt: Mi ___ iz Njemačke. (to be, 1st person plural)
questions.explanation: 'Smo' is the 1st person plural: mi smo = we are.

questions.prompt: Kako se ___? (What is your name? — formal)
questions.hint: This is the formal (Vi) form.
questions.explanation: 'Kako se zovete?' is the formal way to ask someone's name. 'Zovete' is 2nd person plural/formal of 'zvati se'.

questions.prompt: On ___ iz Engleske. (to be, 3rd person singular)
questions.explanation: 'Je' is the 3rd person singular of 'biti'. On je = He is.

questions.prompt: Vi ___ profesori. (to be, 2nd person plural/formal)
questions.explanation: 'Ste' is the 2nd person plural/formal of 'biti'. Vi ste = You are (formal/plural).

questions.prompt: Oni ___ Hrvati. (to be, 3rd person plural)
questions.explanation: 'Su' is the 3rd person plural of 'biti'. Oni su = They are.

questions.prompt: ___ mi je. (Nice to meet you.)
questions.explanation: 'Drago mi je' is the standard expression for 'Nice to meet you.' Drago = dear/pleased.

questions.prompt: Kako se zovete?
questions.explanation: Kako = how, se zovete = are you called (formal). Literally 'How do you call yourself?'

questions.prompt: Drago mi je.
questions.explanation: 'Drago mi je' is a fixed expression meaning 'Nice to meet you' or literally 'It is dear to me'.

questions.prompt: Ja sam iz Hrvatske.
questions.explanation: Ja sam = I am. Iz = from. Hrvatske = Croatia (genitive case).

questions.prompt: On je učitelj.
questions.explanation: On = he, je = is (3rd person singular of biti), učitelj = teacher (male).

questions.prompt: Dobar dan! Kako ste?
questions.explanation: Dobar dan = Good day (standard daytime greeting). Kako ste = How are you (formal).

questions.prompt: Mi smo studenti.
questions.explanation: Mi = we, smo = are (1st person plural), studenti = students (masculine plural).

questions.prompt: I am from Germany.
questions.hint: Remember: countries use the genitive case after 'iz'.
questions.explanation: 'Iz' (from) takes the genitive case. Njemačka → Njemačke.

questions.prompt: Good morning!
questions.explanation: Dobro = good (neuter), jutro = morning (neuter noun, so the adjective matches).

questions.prompt: She is a doctor.
questions.explanation: Ona = she, je = is. Liječnica = female doctor. Doktorica is also acceptable.

questions.prompt: Nice to meet you.
questions.explanation: 'Drago mi je' is the standard expression. Drago = dear/glad, mi = to me, je = is.

questions.prompt: They are from England.
questions.hint: 'From' uses 'iz' + genitive case.
questions.explanation: Oni = they (masc), su = are (3rd plural). Engleska → Engleske in genitive.

questions.prompt: What is your name? (informal)
questions.hint: Use the 'ti' form for informal.
questions.explanation: Zoveš is the 2nd person singular (informal) of 'zvati se'. Compare with formal: zovete.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'biti' (to be) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja sam, ti si, on/ona/ono je, mi smo, vi ste, oni/one/ona su
questions.hint: This is the most important irregular verb in Croatian!
questions.explanation: Biti is completely irregular. These forms must be memorized: sam, si, je, smo, ste, su.
questions.exampleTranslation: I am a student from Zagreb.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'zvati se' (to be called/named) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja se zovem, ti se zoveš, on/ona/ono se zove, mi se zovemo, vi se zovete, oni/one/ona se zovu
questions.hint: This is an -em conjugation verb. Don't forget 'se'!
questions.explanation: Zvati se follows the -em pattern. 'Se' is the reflexive particle and always comes before the verb in this position.
questions.exampleSentence: Kako se zoveš? — Zovem se Ana.
questions.exampleTranslation: What's your name? — My name is Ana.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'govoriti' (to speak) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja govorim, ti govoriš, on/ona/ono govori, mi govorimo, vi govorite, oni/one/ona govore
questions.hint: This is a standard -im conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Govoriti follows the regular -im pattern: -im, -iš, -i, -imo, -ite, -e.
questions.exampleSentence: Govorite li hrvatski? — Da, govorim malo.
questions.exampleTranslation: Do you speak Croatian? — Yes, I speak a little.

questions.prompt: Conjugate the negative of 'biti' (not to be) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja nisam, ti nisi, on/ona/ono nije, mi nismo, vi niste, oni/one/ona nisu
questions.hint: The negative forms of 'biti' are a single word each — 'ni' + the regular form.
questions.explanation: The negative of biti merges 'ne' with the verb: ne + sam → nisam, ne + si → nisi, etc.
questions.exampleSentence: Nisam iz Zagreba, ja sam iz Splita.
questions.exampleTranslation: I'm not from Zagreb, I'm from Split.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'imati' (to have) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja imam, ti imaš, on/ona/ono ima, mi imamo, vi imate, oni/one/ona imaju
questions.hint: This is a standard -am conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Imati follows the regular -am pattern: -am, -aš, -a, -amo, -ate, -aju.
questions.exampleSentence: Imate li pitanje? — Da, imam jedno pitanje.
questions.exampleTranslation: Do you have a question? — Yes, I have one question.

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a masculine noun (e.g. mobitel)?
questions.explanation: Čiji is used for masculine singular nouns. Čija = feminine, Čije = neuter. Mobitel is masculine, so: Čiji je mobitel?

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a feminine noun (e.g. knjiga)?
questions.explanation: Čija is used for feminine singular nouns. Knjiga is feminine, so: Čija je knjiga?

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a neuter noun (e.g. pivo)?
questions.explanation: Čije is used for neuter singular nouns. Pivo is neuter, so: Čije je pivo?

questions.prompt: 'Knjiga je ___.' (The book is mine.) — Choose the correct possessive pronoun (ja).
questions.explanation: Knjiga is feminine singular, so the possessive pronoun must also be feminine: moja. Moj = masculine, moje = neuter.

questions.prompt: 'Računalo je ___.' (The computer is his.) — Choose the correct possessive pronoun (on).
questions.explanation: Računalo is neuter singular, so the possessive must be neuter: njegovo. Njegov = masculine, njegova = feminine.

questions.prompt: 'Automobil je ___.' (The car is ours.) — Choose the correct possessive pronoun (mi).
questions.explanation: Automobil is masculine singular, so the possessive must be masculine: naš. Naša = feminine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: Mobitel je ___. (The phone is mine. — ja, masculine noun)
questions.explanation: Mobitel is masculine, so 'my' must agree: moj. The full sentence: Mobitel je moj.

questions.prompt: Torba je ___. (The bag is ours. — mi, feminine noun)
questions.explanation: Torba is feminine, so 'our' must agree: naša. Naš = masculine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: Beba je ___. (The baby is his. — on, feminine noun)
questions.explanation: Beba is feminine, so 'his' must agree: njegova. Njegov = masculine, njegovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Kuća je ___. (The house is theirs. — oni, feminine noun)
questions.explanation: Kuća is feminine, so 'their' must agree: njihova. Njihov = masculine, njihovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Pivo je ___. (The beer is yours. — Vi, neuter noun)
questions.explanation: Pivo is neuter, so 'your' (formal) must agree: vaše. Vaš = masculine, vaša = feminine.

questions.prompt: Jabuka je ___. (The apple is hers. — ona, feminine noun)
questions.explanation: Jabuka is feminine, so 'her' must agree: njezina. Njezin = masculine, njezino = neuter. Njena/njezina are both used.

questions.prompt: Pecivo je ___. (The pastry is mine. — ja, neuter noun)
questions.explanation: Pecivo is neuter, so 'my' must agree: moje. Moj = masculine, moja = feminine.

questions.prompt: ___ je cipela? (Whose shoe is it? — feminine noun)
questions.explanation: Cipela is feminine, so the question word 'whose' must agree: Čija. Čiji = masculine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ je računalo? (Whose computer is it? — neuter noun)
questions.explanation: Računalo is neuter, so the question word 'whose' must agree: Čije. Čiji = masculine, Čija = feminine.

questions.prompt: Čiji je mobitel?
questions.explanation: Čiji = whose (masculine). Mobitel is masculine, so 'čiji' agrees with it.

questions.prompt: Knjiga je tvoja.
questions.explanation: Tvoja = yours (feminine, matching knjiga). Tvoj = masculine, tvoje = neuter.

questions.prompt: Automobil je njihov.
questions.explanation: Njihov = theirs (masculine, matching automobil). Njihova = feminine, njihovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Kava je njezina.
questions.explanation: Njezina = hers (feminine, matching kava). Njezin = masculine, njezino = neuter.

questions.prompt: The bag is mine. (feminine noun)
questions.hint: Torba is feminine — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Torba (bag) is feminine, so 'my' = moja. Moj = masculine, moje = neuter.

questions.prompt: Whose house is it? (feminine noun)
questions.hint: Kuća is feminine — use the feminine form of 'whose'.
questions.explanation: Kuća is feminine, so 'whose' = čija. Čiji = masculine, čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: The beer is yours. (neuter noun, formal Vi)
questions.hint: Pivo is neuter — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Pivo (beer) is neuter, so 'your' (formal) = vaše. Vaš = masculine, vaša = feminine.

questions.prompt: The computer is his. (neuter noun)
questions.hint: Računalo is neuter — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Računalo (computer) is neuter, so 'his' = njegovo. Njegov = masculine, njegova = feminine.

questions.prompt: The car is ours. (masculine noun)
questions.hint: Automobil is masculine — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Automobil (car) is masculine, so 'our' = naš. Naša = feminine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: 'Knjige su ___.' (The books are mine.) — Choose the correct plural possessive (ja).
questions.explanation: Knjige is feminine plural, so the possessive must be feminine plural: moje. Moji = masculine plural, moja = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: 'Mobiteli su ___.' (The phones are his.) — Choose the correct plural possessive (on).
questions.explanation: Mobiteli is masculine plural, so the possessive must be masculine plural: njegovi. Njegove = feminine plural, njegova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: 'Računala su ___.' (The computers are theirs.) — Choose the correct plural possessive (oni).
questions.explanation: Računala is neuter plural, so the possessive must be neuter plural: njihova. Njihovi = masculine plural, njihove = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a feminine plural noun (e.g. torbe)?
questions.explanation: Čije is used for feminine plural nouns. Čiji = masculine plural, Čija = neuter plural. Torbe is feminine plural, so: Čije su torbe?

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a neuter plural noun (e.g. piva)?
questions.explanation: Čija is used for neuter plural nouns. Note: neuter plural 'whose' looks the same as feminine singular! Čiji = masculine plural, Čije = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Torbe su ___. (The bags are theirs. — one, feminine plural)
questions.explanation: Torbe is feminine plural, so 'their' must agree: njihove. Njihovi = masculine plural, njihova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Bebe su ___. (The babies are hers. — ona, feminine plural)
questions.explanation: Bebe is feminine plural, so 'her' must agree: njezine. Njezini = masculine plural, njezina = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Kuće su ___. (The houses are yours. — Vi, feminine plural)
questions.explanation: Kuće is feminine plural, so 'your' (formal) must agree: vaše. Vaši = masculine plural, vaša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Piva su ___. (The beers are yours. — vi, neuter plural)
questions.explanation: Piva is neuter plural, so 'your' must agree: vaša. Vaši = masculine plural, vaše = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Jabuke su ___. (The apples are his. — on, feminine plural)
questions.explanation: Jabuke is feminine plural, so 'his' must agree: njegove. Njegovi = masculine plural, njegova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Kave su ___. (The coffees are ours. — mi, feminine plural)
questions.explanation: Kave is feminine plural, so 'our' must agree: naše. Naši = masculine plural, naša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Cipele su ___. (The shoes are hers. — ona, feminine plural)
questions.explanation: Cipele is feminine plural, so 'her' must agree: njezine. Njezini = masculine plural, njezina = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Automobili su ___. (The cars are ours. — mi, masculine plural)
questions.explanation: Automobili is masculine plural, so 'our' must agree: naši. Naše = feminine plural, naša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Peciva su ___. (The pastries are mine. — ja, neuter plural)
questions.explanation: Peciva is neuter plural, so 'my' must agree: moja. Moji = masculine plural, moje = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: ___ su mobiteli? (Whose phones are they? — masculine plural)
questions.explanation: Mobiteli is masculine plural, so the question word 'whose' must agree: Čiji. Čije = feminine plural, Čija = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Mobiteli su njegovi.
questions.explanation: Njegovi = his (masculine plural, matching mobiteli). In singular it would be: Mobitel je njegov.

questions.prompt: Čije su torbe?
questions.explanation: Čije = whose (feminine plural, matching torbe). In singular: Čija je torba?

questions.prompt: Računala su njihova.
questions.explanation: Njihova = theirs (neuter plural, matching računala). In singular: Računalo je njihovo.

questions.prompt: Peciva su moja.
questions.explanation: Moja = mine (neuter plural, matching peciva). Note: neuter plural possessive looks like feminine singular (moja).

questions.prompt: The books are mine. (feminine plural)
questions.hint: Knjige is feminine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Knjige (books) is feminine plural, so 'my' = moje. Moji = masculine plural, moja = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: The cars are ours. (masculine plural)
questions.hint: Automobili is masculine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Automobili (cars) is masculine plural, so 'our' = naši. Naše = feminine plural, naša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Whose beers are they? (neuter plural)
questions.hint: Piva is neuter plural — use the neuter plural form of 'whose'.
questions.explanation: Piva (beers) is neuter plural, so 'whose' = čija. Note: čija is both feminine singular and neuter plural!

questions.prompt: The shoes are hers. (feminine plural)
questions.hint: Cipele is feminine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Cipele (shoes) is feminine plural, so 'her' = njezine (or njene). Njezini = masculine plural, njezina = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: The phones are theirs. (masculine plural)
questions.hint: Mobiteli is masculine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Mobiteli (phones) is masculine plural, so 'their' = njihovi. Njihove = feminine plural, njihova = neuter plural.
